title: [CI:DOCS] Add documentation on short-names
body:
Once we settle on the wording for short-names in podman-pull, I will
add the same section to all of the podman commands that use pull.

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]".  That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.
-->

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

Could we stick to one-el `ful` as a suffix? Two-el `full` is nonstandard. I'd love to cite references but have no time this evening, sorry; the best I can do is a [stackexchange post](https://english.stackexchange.com/questions/308917/why-did-ful-prevail-instead-of-full-for-adjectives). If you have a wordlist on your linux box you can do `grep -i '[^-]full$' /usr/share/dict/words` (14 matches on my wordlist) and compare to the one-el form (1084)

@edsantiago Let's talk about this later.  I could swear we had this conversation earlier and we decided to default to rootfull and rootless for the symmetry of the double 'l' and double 's'.  Since both are made up words.
If you google rootful, you get nothing in the top selection about our tools, but if you google rootfull, you get a ton of docs.
I am going to merge, and we can figure out the rootfull versus rootful at some point later.
